location.office_time_required: three days per week in office (Monday, Wednesday, Thursday) starting February 2, 2026; currently minimum two coordinated days (Wednesday and Thursday)

level_signals: 8+ years in SEO, organic growth, or digital marketing
at least 3 years leading a team and owning strategy and budget
Reports to Head of Growth Marketing

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8+ years in SEO, organic growth, or digital marketing, with at least 3 years leading a team and owning strategy and budget at a B2B SaaS, fintech, or technology company.
must_haves.qualifier: with at least 3 years leading a team and owning strategy and budget at a B2B SaaS, fintech, or technology company

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You connect organic channels to pipeline, revenue, and business outcomes, not just traffic and rankings. You think in SQLs, activation rates, and CAC efficiency.
You can read crawl logs, diagnose Core Web Vitals issues, direct schema implementations, and partner credibly with engineers on site architecture decisions.
You've actively experimented with citation strategies, structured content for model consumption, or measured share of voice in answer engines. We can't train this on the job.
You can tell the difference between content that earns trust and content that fills a page. You have experience building or managing content operations at scale with high editorial standards.
You've managed agencies and know how to hold them to outcomes, not just deliverables. You also know when to stop outsourcing and bring work in-house.
You make consequential decisions with incomplete data. You like building a function from scratch more than running one that's already humming.
You treat failure as a feedback loop. You run experiments, kill what doesn't work quickly, and double down on what does.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You've navigated a major Google algorithm update that materially impacted your business and can walk through how you diagnosed, adapted, and recovered.
You understand how retrieval-augmented generation systems select and rank sources, and you can translate that into actionable content and structural strategy.
You use AI and automation tools in your own workflows. You're a practitioner, not just a strategist.
Experience in a regulated or compliance-sensitive industry.

scope.team_size_estimate: unclear (inherits existing team, grows from there)
scope.surface: Organic growth and discoverability across Google, AI answer engines, Reddit, G2, YouTube — end-to-end from first discovery to qualified lead
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS / fintech — corporate cards, expense management, business banking
primary_metrics_inferred: Organic visitor to activated user conversion rate
Share of voice in AI-generated answers
core_responsibilities: Set the organic growth strategy and own the roadmap, OKRs, and budget
Build Brex's AEO/GEO playbook for ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini, Claude, and Google AI Overviews
Co-own the technical SEO roadmap with web engineering
Run content strategy and production for organic
Unify discoverability presence across Reddit, G2, YouTube, and community/review surfaces
Educate sales and partnerships on organic and AI search changes
Hire and develop the organic growth team

compensation.equity_form: equity and other forms of compensation may be provided as part of a total compensation package

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and competitive ($220K–$276K base)
Clear scope of ownership — single accountable owner for all organic/discoverability channels
Explicit acknowledgment of what success looks like in first 12–18 months
Team is lean and AI-native by design — signals high autonomy and modern tooling
Brex has a large balance sheet (Capital One subsidiary) with startup execution speed
Role reports directly to Head of Growth Marketing — high visibility
red_flags: San Francisco office required (hybrid, 3 days/week starting Feb 2026) — eliminates remote candidates
AI visibility fluency explicitly flagged as non-trainable ('We can't train this on the job') — hard filter
Inherits an existing team of unclear composition — potential organizational complexity
Vendor stack rationalization implies prior underperformance or misalignment — may require difficult decisions early
Keyword positions have been lost to competitors in recent algorithm shifts — requires recovery from a deficit
Work auth and visa sponsorship not stated

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Director', years required, and comp range)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities and 'We carry a number' language)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from 'starting base pay will depend on...candidate's location')
ambiguities: Office days requirement: body states currently 2 days (Wednesday/Thursday) but transitions to 3 days (Monday, Wednesday, Thursday) starting February 2, 2026. Both readings captured.
Team size not specified — JD says 'You'll inherit some of it, grow it from there' without numbers.
Equity form is vague — 'equity and other forms of compensation may be provided' does not specify RSUs vs. options.
